close_reason: Already fixed - Sum.toString() uses correct branch: if (ids.isEmpty()) lhs|rhs else ids[lhs|rhs]
description:
File: src/main/kotlin/gh/marad/chi/core/types/Types3.kt:144-151

The non-Option branch of toString() contains a dead ?: branch:

  ids.toString() ?: "$lhs | $rhs"  // ids.toString() is NEVER null

ids is List<TypeId>, so ids.toString() always returns non-null. The ?: branch is unreachable. For anonymous sum types (empty ids), user sees '[]' in type error messages.

Impact: Error messages like 'Expected type is int but got []' instead of 'Expected type is int but got string | float'.

Fix: Replace the else branch:
  if (ids.isEmpty()) "$lhs | $rhs" else "$ids[$lhs | $rhs]"
title: BUG-10: Sum.toString() dead code produces unreadable error messages

description: GitHub issue #20. Add special syntax for defining maps in Chi.
title: Add special syntax to define a map

description: GitHub issue #14. Add concise syntax for defining sets, e.g. {1, 2, 3}.
title: Add special syntax to define a set

close_reason: Already fixed - Type.union() returns Type, no 'as Sum' cast
description:
File: src/main/kotlin/gh/marad/chi/core/types/Types3.kt:48-49

Sum.create flattens and deduplicates types. If all input types collapse to a single type (e.g. Type.union(null, int, int)), create returns a single Primitive, and the 'as Sum' cast throws ClassCastException.

Impact: Internal compiler crash if union is constructed with duplicate types.

Fix: Either guard union() to return Sum only when 2+ distinct types remain, or change the return type to Type and remove the 'as Sum' cast. Check each call site.
title: BUG-11: Type.union() crashes with ClassCastException on duplicate types

close_reason: Already fixed - visitBlock propagates it.used = block.used
description:
File: src/main/kotlin/gh/marad/chi/core/compiler/Usage.kt:69-73

In visitBlock, the last expression is unconditionally marked used=true, ignoring block.used:

  block.body.lastOrNull()?.let {
      it.used = true    // unconditional, ignores block.used
  }

Compare with the correct pattern in visitIfElse which propagates the parent's used flag.

Impact: Affects the Is expression check (Usage.kt:124-131): an 'is' check on a type variable inside a discarded block would incorrectly throw a compile error. Also affects LuaEmitter behavior for expressions that check the used flag.

Fix: Propagate the flag:
  it.used = block.used
title: BUG-12: Block.used flag not propagated — last expression always marked used

close_reason: Already fixed - Handle and EffectDefinition emission is implemented in LuaEmitter
description:
File: src/main/kotlin/gh/marad/chi/lua/LuaEmitter.kt:156-199

The Lua code generation for EffectDefinition and Handle is entirely commented out. The 'when' in emitExpr falls through to:

  else -> TODO("Term $term not supported yet!")

The compiler front-end (parser, type checker) still accepts these constructs without error.

Impact: A Chi program using effect or handle compiles through all front-end phases, then crashes with kotlin.NotImplementedError during Lua emission. No helpful diagnostic.

Fix: Either re-implement the Lua emission for effects/handle, or reject these constructs early with a clear error message in Compiler.kt after AST conversion.
title: BUG-15: Effects/Handle compile but crash at Lua emission

close_reason: Already fixed - visitFunction uses function.copy(types = ...) preserving defaultArgs and typeParams
description:
File: src/main/kotlin/gh/marad/chi/core/types/TypeVisitor.kt:16-17

VariableMapper base class reconstructs Function using only the types field, discarding typeParams and defaultArgs:

  override fun visitFunction(function: Function): Type =
      Function(function.types.map { it.accept(this) })

Function data class has three fields: types, typeParams, defaultArgs. VariableMapper is the base for FreshenAboveVisitor, used by PolyType.instantiate. Every time a polymorphic function type is instantiated, defaultArgs resets to 0 and typeParams are erased.

Impact: Polymorphic functions with default arguments lose their default arg count after instantiation, causing arity errors at call sites where default args are omitted.

Fix: Change to use copy():
  override fun visitFunction(function: Function): Type =
      function.copy(types = function.types.map { it.accept(this) })
title: BUG-05: VariableMapper.visitFunction drops defaultArgs and typeParams

close_reason: Already fixed - visitSum passes typeParams = sum.typeParams to Sum.create
description:
File: src/main/kotlin/gh/marad/chi/core/types/TypeVisitor.kt:24-29

Sum.create call in visitSum does not pass sum.typeParams, so type parameter names are lost during variable mapping (including during FreshenAboveVisitor instantiation). Same class of bug as BUG-05.

Impact: Parametric sum types (e.g. Option[T]) lose their type parameter names when instantiated, which can affect downstream type parameter resolution.

Fix: Pass typeParams to Sum.create:
  Sum.create(
      ids = sum.ids,
      lhs = sum.lhs.accept(this),
      rhs = sum.rhs.accept(this),
      typeParams = sum.typeParams
  )
title: BUG-13: VariableMapper.visitSum drops typeParams

close_reason: Already fixed - debug = false in Constraint.kt
description:
File: src/main/kotlin/gh/marad/chi/core/types/Constraint.kt:5

  private const val debug = true

Every Constraint construction creates a RuntimeException() and captures its stack trace. This happens for every single constraint generated during type inference — hundreds or thousands per program.

Impact: Significant performance degradation during compilation. No functional bug, but noticeable slowdown.

Fix: Change to false, or remove the debug machinery, or gate behind a system property/env variable.
title: BUG-14: Debug stack trace capture always enabled in Constraint

description:
GitHub issue #1. Support destructuring assignments:

For pairs:
  val (a, b) = Pair(1, 2)

For arrays:
  val (head, tail) = arrayOf(1, 2, 3)
  val (first, second, rest) = arrayOf(1, 2, 3)
  val (first, second, third, _) = arrayOf(1, 2, 3)
title: Value destructuring
